text.title: Verb-Object Tool Naming with Namespace Prefix Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the verb-object-tool-naming methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every tool name MUST be lowercase snake_case in the form `verb_object` (e.g. `create_issue`, `search_files`, `apply_patch`). Names of 1 token are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Anthropic internal evals show small renames produce outsized swings; verb_object mirrors CRUD endpoint conventions seen millions of times in pre-training.

rule.statement: When the agent sees tools from more than one service or MCP server, every tool name MUST be prefixed with a service namespace (`github_create_issue`, `stripe_refund_charge`).
rule.rationale: Namespacing eliminates name collisions inside MCP aggregators where multiple servers register `search` or `list`.

rule.statement: Tool names MUST NOT exceed 6 tokens or include marketing nouns (`acme_corp_internal_v2_search_documents_advanced`).
rule.rationale: Long names consume prompt budget and increase lexical-similarity noise across tool pairs.

rule.statement: Renames MUST be data-driven: run eval baseline → rename → rerun eval. Keep the rename only if tool-selection error rate drops measurably.
rule.rationale: Cargo-cult renames degrade catalogs as easily as they fix them; the eval is the source of truth.

rule.statement: Tool name MUST be consistent with `input_schema` arguments and `output_schema` shape: a tool named `search_*` returns a list, `get_*` returns a single record, `*_id` arguments correspond to the entity in the noun.
rule.rationale: Cross-tool consistency lets the model generalize argument-fill across the catalog.
